seniority.level: Senior IC / Staff-level (likely Sr. Manager or Principal)
level_signals: 8+ years of relevant experience listed under 'Strong candidates may have'
Compensation range $300K–$365K base, consistent with senior IC or staff-level at Anthropic
Role involves architecting and scaling a program, reporting to Head of Protective Services
Described as highly strategic; requires cross-functional authority without explicit people management

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience blending management consulting, corporate strategy, or business operations with direct exposure to physical security operations, executive protection, or corporate risk intelligence
Experience building an operations program from scratch in a fast-paced, highly fluid environment
Advanced spreadsheet modeling (Excel / Google Sheets) for capacity, staffing, and budget planning
Strong judgment under pressure; able to make critical operational decisions with incomplete information
Clear written and verbal communication across audiences, including executive leadership
Low ego and high discretion; demonstrated ability to handle highly sensitive personal, travel, and safety data with absolute confidentiality
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience at a high-growth technology company, frontier research organization, or similarly high-scrutiny environment
Stood up protective services or risk management programs in new offices or new countries
Background in corporate threat management, threat assessment teams (WPV), or military/government operations planning
Familiarity with corporate governance and tax treatment of executive protection — SEC perquisite disclosure (Summary Compensation Table) and IRS business-oriented security regulations
Interest in Anthropic's mission and the resilience and safety considerations that come with building frontier AI systems

scope.surface: Executive Protection (EP) program within Global Safety, Intelligence, and Security (GSIS) team
scope.business_model: internal corporate function (non-revenue); security operations enablement
primary_metrics_inferred: KPIs for program readiness and response times
Fiscal health / budget adherence for protective services
Coverage windows and staffing capacity utilization
core_responsibilities: Define, structure, and scale the executive protection program into a proactive corporate function that grows with Anthropic
Translate threat intelligence assessments from the GSIS Intelligence team into concrete, data-backed operational capacity plans — staffing, physical assets, coverage windows
Establish and track KPIs and operational metrics for program readiness, response times, and fiscal health
Develop and maintain repeatable domestic and international travel operational frameworks, including secure-transport playbooks and post-mortems
Partner with the Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) to design communication protocols and alert pipelines for protected individuals
Facilitate selection, acquisition, and maintenance of specialized equipment, vehicles, travel-security applications, and communications technology
Run sourcing, RFP processes, onboarding, and ongoing management of global third-party physical security, secure transport, and intelligence vendors
Own the protective services budget, including in-house vs. contract cost-benefit analyses and quarterly/annual planning reviews
Integrate protective operations into daily corporate workflows by partnering with ABPs, Corporate Travel, Legal, PR/Communications, and IT/InfoSec
Partner with legal counsel on compliance with state licensing laws, corporate insurance policies, and privacy mandates

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($300K–$365K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with best-effort commitment
Clear program ownership and reporting line (Head of Protective Services)
Well-scoped responsibilities with specific deliverables (RFPs, playbooks, KPIs, budget models)
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
No application deadline — rolling basis reduces urgency pressure
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
8+ years of experience listed only under 'Strong candidates may have' (nice-to-have section) but comp range implies seniority expectations are effectively hard
Role blends niche domains (EP operations + corporate strategy + legal/tax compliance) that are rarely combined in one person
No explicit people-management scope stated, which may limit appeal for candidates seeking leadership progression

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range $300K–$365K and program architect framing; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from internal security function context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from KPI responsibility language; not enumerated in JD)
location.cities_listed (inferred as San Francisco from HQ reference; no other city named for this role)
ambiguities: '8+ years of relevant experience' appears under 'Strong candidates may have' (nice-to-have framing) but the compensation range and program architect scope strongly imply it is effectively a hard requirement; classified as soft per JD language but flagged here.
The JD states 'some roles may require more time in our offices' — unclear if this role exceeds the 25% baseline given the in-person security operations context.

manages_people is set to false as the JD does not explicitly mention direct reports, though 'supporting close protection forces' and managing vendor relationships imply significant coordination authority.
